title: paepalanthus aureus
intro: Paepalanthus aureus, also known as the gold button plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Eriocaulaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems, thanks to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Paepalanthus aureus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for its medicinal use, although further scientific research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety.
